stem: Aliyah purchased a new printer for her office. At the time of purchase, the printer had a value of $1,200. Aliyah estimates that each year the printer will depreciate by 25% of the previous year's estimated value. What is the estimated value of the printer exactly 2 years after Aliyah purchased it?
rationale: Choice D is correct. If the printer depreciates by 25% of the previous year's value each year, then each year it retains 100% - 25% = 75%, or 0.75, of the previous year's value. Starting from $1,200, after one year the value is 0.75 × $1,200 = $900, and after two years it is 0.75 × $900 = $675. Equivalently, (0.75)2($1,200) = $675. Choice A is incorrect because $600 would result from depreciating 25% of the original value each year (a flat $300 per year), not 25% of the previous year's value. Choice B is incorrect; this is the result of depreciating 20% per year of the previous value, not 25%. Choice C is incorrect because $900 is the value after only 1 year, not 2 years.
